name: Dogfood Framework Preflight (PR-F4, handover F-013)

design_notes.what_this_feature_is: Feature 043 is the DOGFOOD feature: the framework runs its own shipped tools/ecosystem_preflight.py (feature 041, unmodified) against ITSELF, for real, and lands the resulting evidence as tracked artifacts at .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/{preflight.json,baseline.json} + raw evidence under .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/, per ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md Sec 6 and ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 6. It carries operator_gate=true (feature_list_005.json): a PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS verdict requires real operator approval under gate H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION (product_spec_005.md Sec 8 item 3) before execution continues. This contract authorizes the artifacts and the two-step gated procedure; it does NOT authorize the generator to fabricate or self-supply that approval.
design_notes.expected_real_verdict_and_why: Investigated on the real branch (HEAD 28c8253 at contract-authoring time): git status --porcelain=v1 shows exactly one untracked path today, v0.6.0-release-notes.md (pre-existing, already covered by NDEBT-013/014). Running --self-fixture with --tolerate-untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md and NO operator-approval flags therefore returns exit 2 (PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS, pending) -- confirmed by a disposable-clone dry run in this contract's own verification entries. The real implementation-time run will ALSO observe, as additional untracked exceptions, this very feature's own in-flight .agent/contracts/043.json (and any .agent/qa/043*.json review artifacts) and -- once the pending step has run -- the run's own freshly-created .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/ directory itself (see self_reference_friction below). None of this is a blocking condition under ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md Sec 4 (no tracked file is modified; no required reference is missing) -- it is a genuine, disclosed, exceptions-only PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS, never a FAIL and never fabricated into a bare PASS.
design_notes.two_step_gate_procedure: (1) PENDING STEP: from the repository root, resolve the bounded tolerate-list (see tolerate_list_design below), then invoke `python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id <execution-id> --output-dir .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/pending --self-fixture <resolved --tolerate-untracked flags>` with NO --operator-approver/--operator-authorization. This is expected to exit 2 and write ONLY .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/pending/{preflight.pending.json,collection-log.txt} -- the schema-gated preflight.json is correctly WITHHELD (per tools/ecosystem_preflight.py's own documented exit-2 behavior). The generator MUST STOP here and relay the pending exceptions (the exact contents of preflight.pending.json) to the orchestrator for a REAL human operator decision under gate H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION -- it must not invent an approval itself, and it must not pass any operator-approval flag on this first invocation. (2) APPROVED STEP: ONLY after the orchestrator relays a real, recorded operator decision (identity + verbatim authorization text), the generator re-invokes the SAME CLI with the SAME resolved tolerate-list (recomputed fresh, since the pending step's own output directory is now itself a new untracked path -- see below) PLUS --operator-approver/--operator-authorization carrying the REAL relayed values, with --output-dir .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/approved. This is expected to exit 3 and write preflight.json + baseline.json + collection-log.txt into that approved/ subdirectory. The generator then MOVES (not copies -- see no_evidence_duplication_of_verdict_artifacts; there is exactly one canonical copy of the verdict/baseline, never two) exactly the two schema-gated files -- preflight.json and baseline.json -- out of .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/approved/ into the canonical .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/ location the protocol requires (ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md Sec 6, ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 6), leaving only collection-log.txt behind in approved/ as the raw evidence trail. No third invocation and no --output-dir pointing directly at .agent/reconciliation/ is used, because tools/ecosystem_preflight.py's own --output-dir help text states it 'never defaults into .agent/' by design; placing that hand-off in the calling feature's own implementation step (not the CLI) is exactly what feature 041's contract deferred to a later feature -- this one. (3) DEBT.md BOOKKEEPING (strict ordering, per .agent/qa/043-contract-review.json's A2-hardening request): docs/planning/DEBT.md -- a TRACKED file -- is edited ONLY AFTER both the pending-step and the approved-step invocations have completed and their artifacts are in place; tools/ecosystem_preflight.py's collect_git_clean_state_findings treats ANY modified tracked file as an unconditional blocking finding that no --tolerate-untracked flag can rescue (tolerate-untracked only ever matches '??' untracked lines, never 'M' modified-tracked lines), so editing DEBT.md before either real invocation would guarantee a spurious FAIL and must never happen.
design_notes.execution_id_convention: Pinned formula: `dogfood-<UTC-date:YYYY-MM-DD>-<7-char-short-SHA-of-HEAD-at-run-time>`, e.g. computed at contract-authoring time against the real HEAD as `dogfood-2026-07-17-28c8253` (verified in this contract's own verification entries to collide with nothing already present under .agent/reconciliation/ or .agent/evidence/). The date and SHA are read from a single `date -u +%Y-%m-%d` and `git rev-parse --short=7 HEAD` computed ONCE at the start of the pending step and reused verbatim for the approved step, so both invocations of the same real dogfood execution share one execution_id even though HEAD does not change between them (no commit happens mid-run).
design_notes.tolerate_list_design_and_self_reference_: The --tolerate-untracked flag is an EXACT-match allow-list (tools/ecosystem_preflight.py's collect_git_clean_state_findings does `if repo_relative_path in tolerated`, not a prefix match), so the tolerate-list passed to each invocation must name every currently-untracked path precisely, never a blanket rule. This contract pins a BOUNDED resolution formula (dry-run-proven in verification entry 6, both directions): reading `git status --porcelain=v1`'s `??` lines, a path is tolerated ONLY if it is (a) the single pre-existing, already-debt-registered friction file `v0.6.0-release-notes.md` (NDEBT-013/014), (b) this feature's own in-flight `.agent/contracts/043.json` or an `.agent/qa/043*.json` review artifact (both are this SAME feature's own not-yet-committed lifecycle records, unavoidably present in the working tree by the time implementation runs, since the contract must already be approved before implementation begins), or (c) the run's OWN freshly-created `.agent/evidence/<execution-id>/` directory itself (git reports an entirely-new directory as a single untracked line with a trailing slash; this only ever appears starting with the approved-step invocation, since the pending step is what creates it). ANY OTHER untracked path is NOT swept into the tolerate-list -- the resolution halts loudly (a genuine, correct FAIL) rather than silently laundering an unrelated surprise change into an 'exception'. Empirically confirmed (verification entry 6) with a disposable fixture carrying a fourth, unauthorized untracked file: the bounded resolution correctly refuses and exits non-zero, naming the offending path. This self-reference -- the feature's own in-flight contract/review artifacts AND its own evidence-output directory necessarily appearing as untracked 'exceptions' inside the very artifact they help produce -- is real, newly-discovered friction (empirically reproduced in this contract's own dry runs, not merely theorized) and is registered as new debt (NDEBT-018) per this feature's acceptance test 'Friction registered as debt', rather than being quietly absorbed as if it were ordinary pre-existing debt.
design_notes.ndebt_017_ci_references: tools/ecosystem_preflight.py's own contract (041) non_goals explicitly deferred registering its ci_references honest-limitation (repo-HEAD-anchored, no real CI-run lookup) as its own NDEBT entry to 'a later orchestrator/evaluator action'. This is the first feature that runs the CLI for real against the framework's own tree and lands the resulting baseline.json as a tracked artifact (rather than a scratch-fixture proof), so the limitation is now genuinely exercised, not merely theorized -- registered here as NDEBT-017, referencing this run's own baseline.json/evidence path.
design_notes.honesty_anchoring_head_note: The final committed preflight.json/baseline.json will anchor to the real HEAD at the moment the approved-step invocation ran -- which is necessarily the pre-commit HEAD, since the orchestrator commits this whole feature (contract + artifacts + DEBT.md rows) together in one commit AFTER the run completes, per the established per-feature commit precedent (e.g. commit c8a1e25 for feature 042). This is correct and honest, not a defect: per ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 4/5, a baseline is an immutable, point-in-time snapshot pinning the exact state that was preflighted -- it is never retroactively rewritten to point at a later commit it did not observe. A future Compare step reading this baseline will correctly see the pre-043-commit HEAD as the pinned revision.
design_notes.evidence_vs_reconciliation_split: Two DIFFERENT '.agent/evidence/' conventions coexist under the same parent directory with disjoint naming, so there is no collision: (1) THIS CONTRACT's own verification-command evidence, per methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5, uses the pre-existing FLAT convention `.agent/evidence/043-verify-NN.txt` (unrelated to the dogfood run itself -- these are the captured terminal outputs of the commands in this contract's own 'verification' array). (2) The dogfood RUN's own raw evidence, per ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md Sec 6 / ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 6 / docs/nips/NIP-0001, uses the SUBDIRECTORY convention `.agent/evidence/<execution-id>/{pending,approved}/...` where `<execution-id>` is always prefixed `dogfood-`, never a bare feature number -- so the two conventions can never collide on a shared filename.
design_notes.no_evidence_duplication_of_verdict_artif: RESOLVED (per .agent/qa/043-contract-review.json MEDIUM finding -- this design_note previously contradicted two_step_gate_procedure's now-corrected 'COPIES' wording; the chosen, single, binding reading is NO DUPLICATION). The evidence directory intentionally holds only the RAW, intermediate/as-emitted material (collection-log.txt from each invocation, and the pending step's own preflight.pending.json, which is the only surviving proof of exactly which exceptions were presented to the operator before approval) -- not a second copy of the final preflight.json/baseline.json, which are MOVED (never copied) so they live exactly once, canonically, under .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/. After implementation, .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/approved/ MUST contain collection-log.txt and MUST NOT contain preflight.json or baseline.json (verification entry 'GATE-DEPENDENT: NO DUPLICATE VERDICT ARTIFACTS IN EVIDENCE/' pins this). This keeps the deliverable set to its minimum honest footprint (measured ~130 changed lines; see estimated_lines_derivation) rather than duplicating ~70 lines of JSON that would otherwise sit in two places with no distinct evidentiary value.
design_notes.exception_set_equality_guarantee: RESOLVED (per .agent/qa/043-contract-review.json HIGH finding). The Operator-Exception Rule's entire integrity guarantee (ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md Sec 5) is that a human approves the EXACT exceptions presented to them at the pending step -- not a set the generator (or a later, careless re-invocation) independently re-resolves. This contract therefore pins a binding invariant, checked both as a mechanism-proof (disposable fixtures) and a GATE-DEPENDENT post-implementation counterpart (the real artifacts): reading the pending step's own preflight.pending.json exceptions[] as PENDING_SET (by 'path' identity) and the approved step's preflight.json exceptions[] as APPROVED_SET, the invariant is `APPROVED_SET - PENDING_SET <= {'.agent/evidence/<execution-id>/'}` AND `APPROVED_SET - {'.agent/evidence/<execution-id>/'} == PENDING_SET` -- i.e. the approved set may add EXACTLY ONE well-understood self-reference path (the run's own evidence-output directory, which did not exist yet when the pending step ran) and nothing else; it may never drop a presented exception, and it may never add any other, unpresented path. A drifted approved run (an extra untracked file tolerated only at the approved step, never shown to the operator at pending time) violates this invariant and is caught, not silently accepted as if the operator had reviewed it.
design_notes.estimated_lines_derivation: Measured from disposable-clone dry runs of the exact procedure above: .agent/reconciliation/<id>/preflight.json ~14-26 lines (PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS shape with 2-3 exceptions + operator_approval), .agent/reconciliation/<id>/baseline.json ~47 lines (six single-item reference categories, matching the measured clean-fixture run in this contract's own dry-run proof), .agent/evidence/<id>/pending/preflight.pending.json ~10-15 lines, .agent/evidence/<id>/{pending,approved}/collection-log.txt ~5-6 lines each, docs/planning/DEBT.md +2 lines (NDEBT-017/018 rows, additive only). Total estimate ~130 lines -- a modest buffer over feature_list_005.json's a-priori 120-line estimate, reflecting the baseline.json's six-category shape observed directly rather than assumed.
design_notes.scope_budget_context: Per .agent/run_state.json scope_budget (original_estimate_lines 3500 post-rebaseline, 130% ceiling 4550, cumulative through feature 042 = 3009 lines = 66% of ceiling), this contract's ~130-line estimate leaves substantial headroom; no scope-budget gate applies before this contract.
design_notes.dry_run_proof_summary: All of the following were proven in disposable `cp -a` clones of the real repository (HEAD 28c8253958a2776dd962ee1b18186c908f128129), never touching the real working tree, and are independently re-runnable via this contract's own verification entries: (1) the pending-step invocation against a fixture carrying the real pre-existing friction file plus a simulated in-flight-contract file returns exit 2, writes only the non-schema-conformant preflight.pending.json (independently confirmed to fail jsonschema.validate for a missing required operator_approval), and leaves no preflight.json on disk; (2) the approved-step invocation (same fixture, plus the pending step's own now-untracked output directory correctly added to the tolerate-list) returns exit 3, and both preflight.json and baseline.json validate against their respective schemas with a populated, non-placeholder operator_approval block; (3) a fully clean fixture returns exit 0 with a bare PASS carrying neither an 'exceptions' nor an 'operator_approval' key; (4) the baseline's repository_references/framework_references revision equals a freshly, independently recomputed `git rev-parse HEAD` of the fixture, and its ci_references item carries the honest, non-fabricated limitation note (no run_id, no verified-green claim); (5) the bounded tolerate-list resolution, run against a fixture with a genuinely unauthorized fourth untracked file, correctly refuses to launder it in and exits non-zero, naming the offending path; (6) tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --help documents exactly the flag surface (--execution-id, --output-dir, --repo-root, --self-fixture, --tolerate-untracked, --operator-approver, --operator-authorization, --operator-approved-at) this contract's procedure depends on; (7) the pinned execution-id formula, computed against the REAL repository's actual current HEAD, collides with nothing already present under .agent/reconciliation/ or .agent/evidence/; (8) simulated (not real) .agent/reconciliation/<id>/{preflight.json,baseline.json} and .agent/evidence/<id>/{pending,approved}/* fixtures, built by hand to match the shape this feature's real implementation will produce, prove the GATE-DEPENDENT verification entries' own check logic: a genuine, non-placeholder operator_approval passes while a sentinel-like approver ('test-operator') is caught; a docs/planning/DEBT.md edit that adds exactly two new, correctly-referenced NDEBT rows without altering any pre-existing row passes, and the diff-based check for 'no removed/modified pre-existing line' is itself proven by inspecting `git diff -U0` output. Nothing under the real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework was created, edited, or deleted to produce any of these proofs.

files_create.purpose: The approved (exit-3) preflight verdict artifact, schema-valid against schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, moved (not copied) from the approved-step CLI invocation's --output-dir so it exists exactly once; carries the real, recorded operator_approval block if the verdict is PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS, with an exceptions[] set equal to the pending step's own presented set plus exactly one well-understood self-reference addition (see design_notes.exception_set_equality_guarantee).

The baseline artifact captured by the same approved-step invocation, schema-valid against schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json, anchored to the real HEAD at run time, moved (not copied) from the approved-step CLI invocation's --output-dir so it exists exactly once.
The CLI's own informational, deliberately non-schema-conformant artifact from the pending step, preserved as raw evidence of exactly which exceptions were presented to the operator before approval.
The CLI's own raw collection log from the pending-step invocation.
The CLI's own raw collection log from the approved-step invocation.

files_modify.purpose: Append exactly two new, additive rows -- NDEBT-017 (the ci_references honest-limitation, deferred by contract 041's own non_goals, now exercised for real by this dogfood run) and NDEBT-018 (the dogfood-preflight self-reference friction discovered during this feature's own dry runs: the feature's own in-flight contract/qa artifacts and evidence-output directory necessarily appear as untracked exceptions in their own run) -- each referencing this run's own .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/ path. No existing row is edited, reordered, or removed.

non_goals: No fabrication of operator approval. The generator MUST NOT pass --operator-approver/--operator-authorization on the pending-step invocation, and MUST NOT invent, guess, or self-supply an approval for the approved-step invocation -- it halts after the pending step and relays the exact recorded exceptions to the orchestrator; the approved-step invocation runs only after a real, orchestrator-relayed human decision under gate H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION is in hand.

No execution of gate H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION by this contract or the generator -- only a human operator, via the orchestrator, satisfies that gate; this contract's own dry-run mechanism proofs use clearly-labeled, non-operator sentinel strings (e.g. 'dry-run-mechanism-proof') in disposable fixtures ONLY, never presented as if they were the real approval.

No unbounded or blanket --tolerate-untracked usage -- the tolerate-list passed to each real invocation is resolved by the bounded formula in design_notes.tolerate_list_design_and_self_reference_friction; any untracked path outside that bounded set is a genuine, correct FAIL, not silently tolerated.

No edit to tools/ecosystem_preflight.py, schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json, ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md, ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md, or any other ecosystem/*.md protocol document -- this feature runs the already-shipped, unmodified CLI and protocols; it does not extend them.
No edit to tools/validate.sh, tools/README.md, tools/skill.json, tools/SKILL.md, or NIZAM.json -- feature 042 already wired the validator's ecosystem-fixture coverage; this feature adds no new check.

No creation of tools/compare_ecosystem_baselines.py or tools/validate_evidence_freshness.py -- deferred to later features per docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md's tooling list; not required by any of feature 043's acceptance tests.
No second baseline, audit, findings artifact, or baseline-to-baseline delta -- that is feature 044's (handover F-014) scope entirely.

No duplicate copy of the final preflight.json/baseline.json inside .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/ -- see design_notes.no_evidence_duplication_of_verdict_artifacts; the evidence directory holds only raw/intermediate material.
No edit to any existing docs/planning/DEBT.md row (including NDEBT-001 through NDEBT-016) -- strictly additive rows only.
No resolution of NDEBT-007, NDEBT-009, NDEBT-012, NDEBT-015, or NDEBT-016 -- unrelated, already-catalogued debt, out of this feature's acceptance core.
No edit to CHANGELOG.md -- phase-005 CHANGELOG consolidation is feature 045's (H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE) responsibility, per the established 037/038/039/040/041/042 precedent.
No edit to docs/planning/manifest.json or docs/planning/ROADMAP.md -- unrelated to this feature's acceptance core; ROADMAP updates from dogfood evidence are feature 044's scope.
No edit to .agent/run_state.json -- orchestrator-owned, batched centrally per established precedent.

No edit to any .agent/contracts/*.json other than this 043.json, or any .agent/qa/*.json -- immutable audit records; other concurrently in-flight phase-005 artifacts (034-043 window) are tolerated as untracked additions per the two-part immutability guard, not edited.

No creation, edit, or deletion of the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md -- tolerated (in both the ecosystem-preflight tolerate-list and the scope-guard allow-list) solely to avoid a false-positive failure; never modified by this contract.
No git commit of any kind is performed under this contract or its implementation step -- committing is a separate, later pipeline action.

No execution of any other outstanding phase-005 human gate (H-FRAMEWORK-SCOPE, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE, H-RISK) -- only H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION is in scope, and only its relay/recording, never its self-satisfaction.

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY (HEAD-anchored): no .agent/reconciliation/ directory, no .agent/evidence/dogfood-* directory, and neither NDEBT-017 nor NDEBT-018 exist at HEAD, proving every deliverable is genuinely new.
verification.command: git ls-tree -r HEAD --name-only | grep -q '^\.agent/reconciliation/' && { echo 'a .agent/reconciliation/ path already exists at HEAD -- would be vacuous'; exit 1; }; git ls-tree -r HEAD --name-only | grep -qE '^\.agent/evidence/dogfood-' && { echo 'a .agent/evidence/dogfood-* path already exists at HEAD -- would be vacuous'; exit 1; }; git show HEAD:docs/planning/DEBT.md | grep -qE 'NDEBT-01[78]' && { echo 'NDEBT-017/018 already present at HEAD -- would be vacuous'; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: MECHANISM -- PENDING GATE: in a disposable full clone with the real pre-existing friction file plus a simulated in-flight-contract file, invoking the CLI with the bounded tolerate-list and NO operator flags returns exit 2, writes ONLY the non-schema-conformant preflight.pending.json (confirmed to fail jsonschema.validate for a missing required operator_approval), and leaves preflight.json absent.
verification.command:
T=$(mktemp -d); cp -a . "$T/repo"; ( cd "$T/repo" && git reset --hard -q HEAD && git clean -fdq && echo x > v0.6.0-release-notes.md 2>/dev/null; echo 'fake in-flight contract' > .agent/contracts/043.json && EXEC_ID=dogfood-verify-pending && mkdir -p ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/pending" && python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id "$EXEC_ID" --output-dir ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/pending" --self-fixture --tolerate-untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md --tolerate-untracked .agent/contracts/043.json; rc=$?; [ "$rc" -eq 2 ] || { echo "expected exit 2, got $rc"; exit 1; }; [ -f ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/pending/preflight.pending.json" ] || { echo 'missing preflight.pending.json'; exit 1; }; [ -f ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/pending/preflight.json" ] && { echo 'preflight.json unexpectedly present at pending step'; exit 1; }; python3 -c "
import json, jsonschema
pv = json.load(open('schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json'))
d = json.load(open('.agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/pending/preflight.pending.json'))
try:
    jsonschema.validate(d, pv)
    raise SystemExit('preflight.pending.json unexpectedly schema-valid as a full verdict')
except jsonschema.ValidationError:
    pass
" && echo PASS || exit 1 )

verification.acceptance_test: MECHANISM -- APPROVED GATE: re-invoking the same fixture after the pending step, with the tolerate-list recomputed to also cover the pending step's own now-untracked output directory, plus operator-approval flags, returns exit 3 and both preflight.json/baseline.json validate against their schemas with the exact supplied operator_approval fields (not placeholders).
verification.command:
T=$(mktemp -d); cp -a . "$T/repo"; ( cd "$T/repo" && git reset --hard -q HEAD && git clean -fdq && echo x > v0.6.0-release-notes.md && echo 'fake in-flight contract' > .agent/contracts/043.json && EXEC_ID=dogfood-verify-approved && mkdir -p ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/pending" && python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id "$EXEC_ID" --output-dir ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/pending" --self-fixture --tolerate-untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md --tolerate-untracked .agent/contracts/043.json >/dev/null; mkdir -p ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved"; python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id "$EXEC_ID" --output-dir ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved" --self-fixture --tolerate-untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md --tolerate-untracked .agent/contracts/043.json --tolerate-untracked ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/" --operator-approver jane --operator-authorization 'reviewed exceptions, approved'; rc=$?; [ "$rc" -eq 3 ] || { echo "expected exit 3, got $rc"; exit 1; }; python3 -c "
import json, jsonschema
pv = json.load(open('schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json'))
bv = json.load(open('schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json'))
d = json.load(open('.agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved/preflight.json'))
jsonschema.validate(d, pv)
assert d['operator_approval']['approver'] == 'jane', d
assert d['operator_approval']['authorization'] == 'reviewed exceptions, approved', d
b = json.load(open('.agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved/baseline.json'))
jsonschema.validate(b, bv)
" && echo PASS || exit 1 )

verification.acceptance_test: MECHANISM -- MOVE SEMANTICS (MEDIUM fix, per .agent/qa/043-contract-review.json, resolving the copy-vs-duplicate contradiction as NO DUPLICATION): after the approved-step invocation, moving (not copying) preflight.json/baseline.json into .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/ leaves both schema-valid there, and leaves .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/approved/ with ONLY collection-log.txt -- no duplicate preflight.json/baseline.json remain in the evidence directory.
verification.command:
T=$(mktemp -d); cp -a . "$T/repo"; ( cd "$T/repo" && git reset --hard -q HEAD && git clean -fdq && echo x > v0.6.0-release-notes.md && echo 'fake in-flight contract' > .agent/contracts/043.json && EXEC_ID=dogfood-verify-move && mkdir -p ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/pending" && python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id "$EXEC_ID" --output-dir ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/pending" --self-fixture --tolerate-untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md --tolerate-untracked .agent/contracts/043.json >/dev/null; mkdir -p ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved"; python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id "$EXEC_ID" --output-dir ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved" --self-fixture --tolerate-untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md --tolerate-untracked .agent/contracts/043.json --tolerate-untracked ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/" --operator-approver jane --operator-authorization approved >/dev/null; mkdir -p ".agent/reconciliation/${EXEC_ID}"; mv ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved/preflight.json" ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved/baseline.json" ".agent/reconciliation/${EXEC_ID}/"; python3 -c "
import json, jsonschema
pv = json.load(open('schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json'))
bv = json.load(open('schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json'))
jsonschema.validate(json.load(open('.agent/reconciliation/${EXEC_ID}/preflight.json')), pv)
jsonschema.validate(json.load(open('.agent/reconciliation/${EXEC_ID}/baseline.json')), bv)
" && [ ! -f ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved/preflight.json" ] && [ ! -f ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved/baseline.json" ] && [ -f ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved/collection-log.txt" ] && echo PASS || { echo "move-semantics check failed: duplicate(s) remained in evidence/approved/, or reconciliation artifacts missing/invalid"; exit 1; } )

verification.acceptance_test: MECHANISM -- EXCEPTION-SET EQUALITY, BOTH DIRECTIONS (HIGH fix, per .agent/qa/043-contract-review.json): a non-drifted run's approved exceptions[] equals the pending exceptions[] plus exactly the one well-understood self-reference addition (PASS); a drifted run -- an extra untracked file tolerated only at the approved step, never presented to the operator at pending time -- correctly FAILS this same invariant, proving the check would catch it rather than passing it silently (as the un-fixed contract's entry 3 was shown to).
verification.command:
GOOD_OK=0; T1=$(mktemp -d); cp -a . "$T1/repo"; ( cd "$T1/repo" && git reset --hard -q HEAD && git clean -fdq && echo x > v0.6.0-release-notes.md && echo 'fake in-flight contract' > .agent/contracts/043.json && EXEC_ID=dogfood-verify-excset-good && mkdir -p ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/pending" && python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id "$EXEC_ID" --output-dir ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/pending" --self-fixture --tolerate-untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md --tolerate-untracked .agent/contracts/043.json >/dev/null; mkdir -p ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved"; python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id "$EXEC_ID" --output-dir ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved" --self-fixture --tolerate-untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md --tolerate-untracked .agent/contracts/043.json --tolerate-untracked ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/" --operator-approver jane --operator-authorization approved >/dev/null; python3 -c "
import json
EXEC_ID = '${EXEC_ID}'
pending = json.load(open(f'.agent/evidence/{EXEC_ID}/pending/preflight.pending.json'))
approved = json.load(open(f'.agent/evidence/{EXEC_ID}/approved/preflight.json'))
pset = {e['path'] for e in pending.get('exceptions', [])}
aset = {e['path'] for e in approved.get('exceptions', [])}
allowed = {f'.agent/evidence/{EXEC_ID}/'}
assert aset - pset <= allowed, ('unexpected extra exception(s)', aset - pset)
assert (aset - allowed) == pset, ('approved set (minus self-reference dir) != pending set', aset, pset)
" ) && GOOD_OK=1 || GOOD_OK=0; DRIFT_CAUGHT=0; T2=$(mktemp -d); cp -a . "$T2/repo"; ( cd "$T2/repo" && git reset --hard -q HEAD && git clean -fdq && echo x > v0.6.0-release-notes.md && echo 'fake in-flight contract' > .agent/contracts/043.json && EXEC_ID=dogfood-verify-excset-drift && mkdir -p ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/pending" && python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id "$EXEC_ID" --output-dir ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/pending" --self-fixture --tolerate-untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md --tolerate-untracked .agent/contracts/043.json >/dev/null; echo 'drifted, never shown to the operator' > .agent/contracts/999-drift.json; mkdir -p ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved"; python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id "$EXEC_ID" --output-dir ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved" --self-fixture --tolerate-untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md --tolerate-untracked .agent/contracts/043.json --tolerate-untracked ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/" --tolerate-untracked .agent/contracts/999-drift.json --operator-approver alice.chen --operator-authorization 'reviewed and approved the two pending exceptions' >/dev/null; python3 -c "
import json
EXEC_ID = '${EXEC_ID}'
pending = json.load(open(f'.agent/evidence/{EXEC_ID}/pending/preflight.pending.json'))
approved = json.load(open(f'.agent/evidence/{EXEC_ID}/approved/preflight.json'))
pset = {e['path'] for e in pending.get('exceptions', [])}
aset = {e['path'] for e in approved.get('exceptions', [])}
allowed = {f'.agent/evidence/{EXEC_ID}/'}
assert aset - pset <= allowed, ('unexpected extra exception(s)', aset - pset)
assert (aset - allowed) == pset, ('approved set (minus self-reference dir) != pending set', aset, pset)
" ) && DRIFT_CAUGHT=0 || DRIFT_CAUGHT=1; if [ "$GOOD_OK" -eq 1 ] && [ "$DRIFT_CAUGHT" -eq 1 ]; then echo PASS; else echo "FAILED: GOOD_OK=$GOOD_OK DRIFT_CAUGHT=$DRIFT_CAUGHT"; exit 1; fi

verification.acceptance_test: MECHANISM -- CLEAN PASS: a fully clean disposable fixture yields exit 0, and the emitted preflight.json carries neither an 'exceptions' nor an 'operator_approval' key.
verification.command:
T=$(mktemp -d); mkdir -p "$T/schema"; cp schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json "$T/schema/"; ( cd "$T" && git init -q && git config user.email a@a.com && git config user.name a && git add -A && git commit -q -m init ); OUT=$(mktemp -d); python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id dogfood-verify-clean --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$T"; rc=$?; [ "$rc" -eq 0 ] || { echo "expected exit 0, got $rc"; exit 1; }; python3 -c "
import json, jsonschema
pv = json.load(open('schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json'))
d = json.load(open('$OUT/preflight.json'))
jsonschema.validate(d, pv)
assert d['verdict'] == 'PASS'
assert 'exceptions' not in d
assert 'operator_approval' not in d
" && echo PASS || exit 1

verification.acceptance_test: HONESTY ANCHOR: the approved fixture's baseline.json repository_references/framework_references revision equals the fixture's own freshly-recomputed git rev-parse HEAD, and its ci_references item carries a genuine, non-fabricated limitation note (no run_id, no verified-green claim).
verification.command:
T=$(mktemp -d); cp -a . "$T/repo"; ( cd "$T/repo" && git reset --hard -q HEAD && git clean -fdq && echo x > v0.6.0-release-notes.md && REAL_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD) && EXEC_ID=dogfood-verify-honesty && mkdir -p ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved" && python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id "$EXEC_ID" --output-dir ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved" --self-fixture --tolerate-untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md --operator-approver jane --operator-authorization approved >/dev/null; python3 -c "
import json
b = json.load(open('.agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved/baseline.json'))
real_head = '$REAL_HEAD'
assert b['repository_references'][0]['revision'] == real_head, (b['repository_references'][0]['revision'], real_head)
assert b['framework_references'][0]['revision'] == real_head
ci = b['ci_references'][0]
note = ci.get('note', '')
assert isinstance(note, str) and note.strip()
assert 'run_id' not in ci
assert ci.get('status') != 'verified-green'
" && echo PASS || exit 1 )

verification.acceptance_test: BOUNDED TOLERATE-LIST LOAD-BEARING PROOF: the pinned resolution formula (design_notes.tolerate_list_design_and_self_reference_friction) tolerates exactly the pre-existing friction file, this feature's own in-flight contract, and its own qa-review artifact -- but correctly REFUSES to launder in a fourth, unauthorized untracked file, halting loudly rather than silently sweeping it in.
verification.command: T=$(mktemp -d); cp -a . "$T/repo"; ( cd "$T/repo" && git reset --hard -q HEAD && git clean -fdq && echo x > v0.6.0-release-notes.md && echo y > .agent/contracts/043.json && echo z > .agent/qa/043-contract-review.json && echo surprise > oops_unrelated.txt && UNTRACKED=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | awk '/^\?\? / {print substr($0,4)}'); bad=0; for p in $UNTRACKED; do case "$p" in v0.6.0-release-notes.md|.agent/contracts/043.json|.agent/qa/043*.json) ;; *) echo "correctly refused to tolerate: $p"; bad=1 ;; esac; done; [ "$bad" -eq 1 ] && echo PASS || { echo 'FAILED to catch the unauthorized untracked path'; exit 1; } )

verification.acceptance_test: CLI FLAG-SURFACE ANCHOR: tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --help documents exactly the flags this contract's two-step procedure depends on, anchored to the real shipped binary (not assumed from reading feature 041's contract).
verification.command: h=$(python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --help 2>&1); for f in --execution-id --output-dir --repo-root --self-fixture --tolerate-untracked --operator-approver --operator-authorization --operator-approved-at; do echo "$h" | grep -Fq -- "$f" || { echo "missing documented flag: $f"; exit 1; }; done; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: EXECUTION-ID FORMULA DETERMINISM + NON-COLLISION: the pinned formula, computed against the REAL repository's current HEAD, produces a syntactically well-formed, non-empty id that collides with nothing already present under .agent/reconciliation/ or .agent/evidence/.
verification.command: EXEC_ID="dogfood-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)-$(git rev-parse --short=7 HEAD)"; echo "$EXEC_ID" | grep -Eq '^dogfood-[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9a-f]{7}$' || { echo "malformed execution-id: $EXEC_ID"; exit 1; }; [ -e ".agent/reconciliation/${EXEC_ID}" ] && { echo 'collision under .agent/reconciliation/'; exit 1; }; [ -e ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}" ] && { echo 'collision under .agent/evidence/'; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: GATE-DEPENDENT (post-implementation): the real .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/preflight.json exists, validates against schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, and (if PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS) carries a non-empty operator_approval.approver/.authorization that is NOT a dry-run sentinel string.
verification.command:
EXEC_ID=$(basename $(ls -dt .agent/reconciliation/dogfood-* 2>/dev/null | head -1) 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$EXEC_ID" ] || { echo 'no .agent/reconciliation/dogfood-* directory found yet -- expected before implementation lands, must exist and validate after'; exit 1; }; python3 -c "
import json, jsonschema
pv = json.load(open('schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json'))
d = json.load(open('.agent/reconciliation/${EXEC_ID}/preflight.json'))
jsonschema.validate(d, pv)
if d['verdict'] == 'PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS':
    oa = d.get('operator_approval', {})
    approver, auth = oa.get('approver',''), oa.get('authorization','')
    SENTINELS = {'dry-run-mechanism-proof','test-operator','test','jane',''}
    assert approver and approver not in SENTINELS, ('placeholder-looking approver', approver)
    assert auth and auth not in SENTINELS, ('placeholder-looking authorization', auth)
" && echo PASS || exit 1

verification.acceptance_test: GATE-DEPENDENT (post-implementation, HIGH fix per .agent/qa/043-contract-review.json): the real .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/preflight.json's exceptions[] equals the real pending step's .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/pending/preflight.pending.json exceptions[] plus at most the one well-understood self-reference addition -- proving the human operator approved exactly what was presented, not a silently drifted superset.
verification.command:
EXEC_ID=$(basename $(ls -dt .agent/reconciliation/dogfood-* 2>/dev/null | head -1) 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$EXEC_ID" ] || { echo "no .agent/reconciliation/dogfood-* directory found yet"; exit 1; }; [ -f ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/pending/preflight.pending.json" ] || { echo "missing pending/preflight.pending.json -- cannot verify exception-set equality"; exit 1; }; python3 -c "
import json
EXEC_ID = '$EXEC_ID'
pending = json.load(open(f'.agent/evidence/{EXEC_ID}/pending/preflight.pending.json'))
approved = json.load(open(f'.agent/reconciliation/{EXEC_ID}/preflight.json'))
pset = {e['path'] for e in pending.get('exceptions', [])}
aset = {e['path'] for e in approved.get('exceptions', [])}
allowed = {f'.agent/evidence/{EXEC_ID}/'}
assert aset - pset <= allowed, ('unexpected extra exception(s) never presented at the pending step', aset - pset)
assert (aset - allowed) == pset, ('approved set (minus the known self-reference dir) does not equal the pending set', aset, pset)
" && echo PASS || exit 1

verification.acceptance_test: GATE-DEPENDENT (post-implementation): the real .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/baseline.json exists, validates against schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json, and its repository_references/framework_references revision equals a freshly recomputed git rev-parse HEAD of the real repository.
verification.command:
EXEC_ID=$(basename $(ls -dt .agent/reconciliation/dogfood-* 2>/dev/null | head -1) 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$EXEC_ID" ] || { echo 'no .agent/reconciliation/dogfood-* directory found yet'; exit 1; }; REAL_HEAD=$(git rev-parse HEAD); python3 -c "
import json, jsonschema
bv = json.load(open('schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json'))
b = json.load(open('.agent/reconciliation/${EXEC_ID}/baseline.json'))
jsonschema.validate(b, bv)
real_head = '$REAL_HEAD'
assert b['repository_references'][0]['revision'] == real_head
assert b['framework_references'][0]['revision'] == real_head
" && echo PASS || exit 1

verification.acceptance_test: GATE-DEPENDENT (post-implementation): evidence is stored by path -- .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/pending/ and /approved/ both exist and are non-empty, per ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md Sec 6 / ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 6.
verification.command: EXEC_ID=$(basename $(ls -dt .agent/evidence/dogfood-* 2>/dev/null | head -1) 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$EXEC_ID" ] || { echo 'no .agent/evidence/dogfood-* directory found yet'; exit 1; }; test -s ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/pending/collection-log.txt" && test -s ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/pending/preflight.pending.json" && test -s ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved/collection-log.txt" && echo PASS || { echo 'evidence directory missing expected raw files'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: GATE-DEPENDENT (post-implementation, MEDIUM fix per .agent/qa/043-contract-review.json, resolving the copy-vs-duplicate contradiction as NO DUPLICATION): .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/approved/ contains ONLY collection-log.txt -- no duplicate preflight.json or baseline.json remain there after the move into .agent/reconciliation/.
verification.command: EXEC_ID=$(basename $(ls -dt .agent/evidence/dogfood-* 2>/dev/null | head -1) 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$EXEC_ID" ] || { echo "no .agent/evidence/dogfood-* directory found yet"; exit 1; }; [ -f ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved/preflight.json" ] && { echo "duplicate preflight.json unexpectedly present under .agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved/ -- should have been MOVED, not copied"; exit 1; }; [ -f ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved/baseline.json" ] && { echo "duplicate baseline.json unexpectedly present under .agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved/ -- should have been MOVED, not copied"; exit 1; }; test -s ".agent/evidence/${EXEC_ID}/approved/collection-log.txt" && echo PASS || { echo "approved/collection-log.txt missing or empty"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: GATE-DEPENDENT (post-implementation): friction is registered as debt -- docs/planning/DEBT.md carries exactly two new rows (NDEBT-017 and NDEBT-018), each referencing this run's own .agent/evidence/ path, and no pre-existing TABLE ROW (up to NDEBT-016) was altered or removed (the check is scoped to removed table-row lines, i.e. lines matching '^-|', so the contracted frontmatter version-bump line is correctly not mistaken for a row alteration).
verification.command: diff_out=$(git diff -U0 -- docs/planning/DEBT.md); removed=$(echo "$diff_out" | grep -E '^-\|' | grep -v '^---'); [ -z "$removed" ] || { echo "an existing DEBT.md row was altered/removed:"; echo "$removed"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'NDEBT-017' docs/planning/DEBT.md && grep -q 'NDEBT-018' docs/planning/DEBT.md || { echo 'NDEBT-017/018 not both present'; exit 1; }; grep -A0 'NDEBT-017' docs/planning/DEBT.md | grep -q '.agent/evidence/' && grep -A0 'NDEBT-018' docs/planning/DEBT.md | grep -q '.agent/evidence/' || { echo 'NDEBT-017/018 rows do not reference the evidence path'; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: NON-GOAL ENFORCEMENT: no out-of-scope surface (schema/, tools/, ecosystem/, NIZAM.json, CHANGELOG.md, docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, .agent/run_state.json) was modified.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- schema/ tools/ ecosystem/ NIZAM.json CHANGELOG.md docs/planning/manifest.json docs/planning/ROADMAP.md .agent/run_state.json && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope path was modified"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- schema/ tools/ ecosystem/ NIZAM.json CHANGELOG.md docs/planning/manifest.json docs/planning/ROADMAP.md .agent/run_state.json; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: SCOPE GUARD (untracked-aware): outside .agent/, only docs/planning/DEBT.md changed, plus the pre-existing tolerated untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'docs/planning/DEBT.md' 'v0.6.0-release-notes.md' && echo PASS || { echo "out-of-scope change(s) detected"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: .agent/ immutability guard (two-part invariant, per 038/040/041/042 precedent, glob range extended to cover the current phase-005 in-flight window 034-043, plus this feature's own new .agent/reconciliation/ and .agent/evidence/dogfood-* additions): (a) no TRACKED/HISTORICAL .agent/qa or .agent/contracts record was modified; (b) every UNTRACKED addition under .agent/qa, .agent/contracts, .agent/reconciliation, or .agent/evidence is confined to a legitimately in-flight phase-005 feature or this feature's own dogfood-run artifacts.
verification.command: bad=0; if ! git diff --quiet HEAD -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts; then echo "tracked/historical .agent/qa or .agent/contracts record modified:"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts; bad=1; fi; while IFS= read -r line; do case "$line" in "?? "*) path="${line:3}"; case "$path" in .agent/contracts/03[4-9]*.json|.agent/contracts/04[0-3]*.json|.agent/qa/03[4-9]*.json|.agent/qa/04[0-3]*.json|.agent/reconciliation/dogfood-*|.agent/evidence/dogfood-*|.agent/evidence/043-verify-*.txt|.agent/evidence/043/*) ;; *) echo "out-of-scope untracked .agent artifact: $path"; bad=1 ;; esac ;; esac; done < <(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts .agent/reconciliation .agent/evidence); if [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo PASS; else exit 1; fi

verification.acceptance_test: F-013/AT#8 (literal, feature_list_005.json, BLOCKER-1 backfill per Mode B 043 review): the compliance validator stays green -- bash tools/validate.sh exits 0 and its final SUMMARY line reads '<N> passed, 0 failed' (count-agnostic, matching the feature-list AT's literal wording; current shipped count is 12).
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; [ "$rc" -eq 0 ] && echo "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Eq '^SUMMARY: [0-9]+ passed, 0 failed$' && echo PASS || { echo "validator sweep did not exit 0 with a clean SUMMARY"; echo "$out" | tail -3; exit 1; }

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/043-verify-NN.txt (the pre-existing FLAT per-contract convention, methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5 -- distinct from, and never colliding with, the dogfood run's own .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/{pending,approved}/ subdirectory convention this contract's deliverables create; see design_notes.evidence_vs_reconciliation_split). NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (the 'verification' array has exactly 20 entries, so .agent/evidence/043-verify-01.txt through 043-verify-20.txt). Every evidence file MUST comply with the Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.

approvals.round0_disposition: REJECTED at revision 0 (one HIGH finding, one MEDIUM finding; three Mode-A advisories reproduced and concurred non-blocking). HIGH (exception-set drift, verification_index 3): no entry asserted that the approved artifact's exceptions[] equals the pending step's presented exceptions[] -- the evaluator built a drifted approved run (an extra untracked file tolerated only at the approved step, never shown to the operator at pending time) that still passed entry 3's literal check end-to-end, undermining the Operator-Exception Rule's core guarantee. FIX (this revision): added a new mechanism-proof entry 'MECHANISM -- EXCEPTION-SET EQUALITY, BOTH DIRECTIONS' (both a clean, non-drifted case passing and a drifted case correctly failing, re-proven on fresh disposable clones) and a GATE-DEPENDENT post-implementation counterpart comparing the real .agent/evidence/<id>/pending/preflight.pending.json exceptions[] against the real .agent/reconciliation/<id>/preflight.json exceptions[], permitting only the one well-understood self-reference addition (the run's own evidence-output directory). MEDIUM (copy-vs-duplicate contradiction): design_notes.two_step_gate_procedure claimed the approved-step artifacts were COPIED (preserved) into evidence/approved/, directly contradicting design_notes.no_evidence_duplication_of_verdict_artifacts and scope.files_create, neither of which any verification entry pinned either way. FIX (this revision): resolved to NO DUPLICATION -- two_step_gate_procedure now reads MOVES, not copies; no_evidence_duplication_of_verdict_artifacts marked RESOLVED; a new mechanism-proof entry 'MECHANISM -- MOVE SEMANTICS' and a GATE-DEPENDENT counterpart '... NO DUPLICATE VERDICT ARTIFACTS IN EVIDENCE/' both pin that .agent/evidence/<id>/approved/ contains only collection-log.txt after the hand-off. CHEAP HARDENING (evaluator-recommended, A2): added one explicit sentence to two_step_gate_procedure pinning that docs/planning/DEBT.md -- a tracked file -- is edited only AFTER both preflight invocations complete, since a modified tracked file is an unconditional blocking finding no --tolerate-untracked flag can rescue. Verification entries grew from 15 to 19 (four new: two mechanism-proofs, two GATE-DEPENDENT counterparts). All entries re-run verbatim (extracted from this JSON via json.load) against fresh disposable `cp -a` clones of real HEAD 28c8253958a2776dd962ee1b18186c908f128129 -- the 15 mechanism-provable/structural entries PASS as written; the (now 6, previously 4) GATE-DEPENDENT post-implementation entries correctly and expectedly FAIL pre-implementation (no real dogfood run has occurred yet), matching the established anti-vacuity pattern. Nothing under the real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework was created, edited, or deleted to produce any of these revision proofs, other than this contract file itself; every operator-approver/-authorization value used in any proof was a clearly-labeled test string ('jane', 'alice.chen', 'test', etc.) inside disposable fixtures only. status remains 'proposed'; scope.files_create/files_modify and non_goals are otherwise unchanged; estimated_lines (130) is unchanged, since these are contract-verification-array additions, not feature-deliverable growth.

amendments.target: verification[16] ("friction is registered as debt", GATE-DEPENDENT)
amendments.change: Narrowed the 'no pre-existing row altered/removed' check from a blanket 'grep -E "^-"' (any removed diff line) to 'grep -E "^-\\|"' (only removed table-row lines), and clarified the acceptance_test wording accordingly.
amendments.rationale: As originally written, the check would have false-failed against the coordinator-instructed, house-convention DEBT.md frontmatter version bump (0.10.0 -> 0.11.0, matching the identical precedent in commits 17408d9 and 28c8253 for the immediately preceding NDEBT-013/014 and NDEBT-015/016 registrations) -- a legitimate '-version: 0.10.0' / '+version: 0.11.0' diff pair is not a table-ROW alteration, which is what this check (and non_goals' 'no existing row is edited, reordered, or removed') actually guards against. Discovered empirically when running the real implementation diff through the unmodified check.
amendments.dry_run_proof: git diff -U0 -- docs/planning/DEBT.md on the real implementation diff shows exactly one '-' line ('-version: 0.10.0') and one '+' line ('+version: 0.11.0') plus the two new '+' table rows (NDEBT-017/018); the original blanket check would have flagged the version '-' line as a false-positive 'row altered' failure, while the narrowed '^-\|' pattern correctly reports zero removed table-row lines and PASSes.
amendments.ratified_by_mode_b: pending -- to be confirmed by validator Mode B this review pass

amendments.target: verification entries 2/3/4/5/7 (disposable-clone mechanism proofs) and verification[19] (.agent/ immutability guard)
amendments.change: (a) Entries 2/3/4/5/7's disposable-clone setup now runs 'git reset --hard -q HEAD && git clean -fdq' (previously 'git clean -fdq' alone), so the clone also discards any TRACKED modification inherited from 'cp -a .', not only untracked files. (b) verification[19]'s .agent/evidence glob allowlist now also recognizes '.agent/evidence/043-verify-*.txt' and '.agent/evidence/043/*' (the pre-existing FLAT per-contract verification-evidence convention this contract's own evidence_convention requires).
amendments.rationale: (a) Discovered empirically running the real implementation's full verification sweep: after docs/planning/DEBT.md was edited (a legitimate, contracted, TRACKED modification), a disposable clone's 'cp -a .' step inherited that same uncommitted tracked change; 'git clean -fdq' only removes untracked files, so the clone's own pending/approved CLI invocations correctly (but disruptively for the proof) reported 'uncommitted tracked change (M): docs/planning/DEBT.md' as blocking, breaking entries whose fixtures assume a clean HEAD baseline before introducing their OWN controlled untracked files. (b) Discovered empirically that entry 19, run for real after evidence capture began, correctly-by-its-own-logic flagged the very .agent/evidence/043-verify-NN.txt and .agent/evidence/043/verification.txt files this contract's evidence_convention REQUIRES as 'out-of-scope untracked .agent artifact' -- the glob allowlist had no entry for the pre-existing flat per-contract evidence-file convention (distinct from the dogfood run's own .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/ subdirectory convention, which was already allowed).
amendments.dry_run_proof: Both fixes re-verified against the real implementation state: with (a), entries 2/3/4/5/7 all PASS again (disposable clones ignore the real repo's in-progress DEBT.md edit). With (b), entry 19 PASSes once the flat evidence files exist, and -- re-checked negatively -- a genuinely out-of-scope untracked path (e.g. a stray .agent/qa/999-bogus.json) is still correctly caught by the unchanged remainder of the glob.
amendments.ratified_by_mode_b: pending -- to be confirmed by validator Mode B this review pass

amendments.change: Added verification entry 20: runs 'bash tools/validate.sh', asserts exit 0 and a final SUMMARY line matching '^SUMMARY: [0-9]+ passed, 0 failed$' (count-agnostic, matching feature_list_005.json F-013 AT#8's literal wording; current shipped count is 12). evidence_convention updated from 19 to 20 entries.
amendments.rationale: Mode B (043 review) found BLOCKER-1: feature_list_005.json's AT#8 ('Compliance validator stays green') had no corresponding contract verification entry and no captured evidence -- a Mode-A coverage gap the validator itself acknowledged under-catching at contract-review time. Orchestrator authorized this narrow backfill amendment (same amendment mechanics as amendments 1/2).
amendments.dry_run_proof: Executed for real against the actual implemented tree (not a scratch fixture, since this AT is about the shipped validator's live state): 'bash tools/validate.sh' exits 0, final line 'SUMMARY: 12 passed, 0 failed', matching the count-agnostic regex. Captured to .agent/evidence/043-verify-20.txt.
